Import Keras and other necessary libraries from the pointnet.py file
Download and prepare the disaster-related tweets dataset from Kaggle
Shuffle the dataset to ensure randomness in data splitting
Drop unnecessary columns 'id', 'keyword', and 'location' from the dataframe
Split the shuffled dataset into training (90%) and testing (10%) sets using a random state of 42
Create a function `create_dataset` that takes a dataframe, converts it into a `tf.data.Dataset`, batches it with size 100, and prefetches data automatically
Use the `create_dataset` function to create training (`train_ds`) and testing (`test_ds`) datasets from the split dataframes
Define the input shape for the model as (None, ) to handle variable-length text inputs
Create a Universal Sentence Encoder layer using TensorFlow Hub with the specified URL
Build `model_1` by creating an input layer of type tf.string and passing it through the preprocessor consisting of the Universal Sentence Encoder layer
Define `model_2` directly using the Gradient Boosted Trees model from Tensorflow Decision Forests (TF-DF)
Compile both models with metrics 'Accuracy', 'Recall', 'Precision', and 'AUC'
Train `model_1` and `model_2` on the training dataset (`train_ds`) without specifying epochs, as TF-DF trains exactly one epoch of the dataset
Evaluate both models on the testing dataset (`test_ds`) and capture the results in a dictionary format to return precision, recall, and other metrics
